name: Salmon Steaks w/ Black Bean Sauce
recipeIngredient: 6 Green onions, 6 slices of fresh peeled ginger (quarter-sized)
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak black beans in lukewarm water for 5 minutes.
2. Drain and rinse with cold water; drain again.
3. Combine black beans with garlic, ginger, soy sauce, dry vermouth, and sugar. Gently mash together.
4. Cut green onions into 2-inch-long sections and cut some into 2-inch-long slivers.
5. Place green onion sections and half the shredded ginger at the bottom of a shallow heatproof plate (like a glass Pyrex pie plate).
6. Sprinkle salt over salmon steaks.
7. Place salmon steaks in a single layer on top of the green onions.
8. If needed, use two plates with a 2-tier steamer unit.
9. Scatter remaining shredded ginger.
10. Top each steak with half a tablespoon of the black-bean mixture and an equal amount of green onion slivers.
11. Fill a wok or steamer with enough boiling water to come within 1 inch of the bottom of the bamboo steamer.
12. When the water comes to a boil, place the fish with its plate into the bamboo steamer. Cover.
13. Steam over medium-high heat for 10 minutes.
14. Remove cover away from your face and carefully lift out the plate.
15. In a small pan, heat peanut or corn oil until hot and almost smoking.
16. Sprinkle salmon with white pepper and scatter remaining fresh green onion slivers on top.
17. Carefully pour the hot oil over the fish steaks. It should sizzle.
18. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves.
19. Serve hot with steamed rice.
